obligation_text: Before placing a high-risk AI system on the market, importers shall ensure that: the appropriate conformity assessment procedure has been carried out by the provider; the provider has drawn up the technical documentation; the system bears the required CE marking and is accompanied by the EU declaration of conformity and instructions for use.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system import compliance requirements, including verification of provider conformity assessment, technical documentation assessment, CE marking verification, and declaration of conformity and instructions review.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system import compliance requirements, including verification of provider conformity assessment, technical documentation assessment, CE marking verification, and declaration of conformity and instructions review.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 23 (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.672, cosine: 0.785). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.767), AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.752).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.7/10 (raw 0.672). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity, documentation, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [ce, declaration, import, instructions, marking, provider] vs WIDAI terms [act, categories, eu, high, obligations, prohibited] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.900 (P=0.894, R=0.906). Balanced P/R — the 26-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.785 > STS 0.672 (gap +0.113). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity, documentation] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 32.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity, documentation, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [ce, declaration, import, instructions, marking, provider]. WIDAI-only: [act, categories, eu, high, obligations, prohibited].
